ip_outcome_note: Card frame names 'Opal' and the depicted figure matches Opal's canonical character design: elderly woman in tall pointed wide-brimmed hat, layered deep-purple gown, pink-tinged hair, leaning on a curved cane, in a Ballonlea-style bauble-lit performance setting. Print art by take.

trivia_bullets.text: Opal is the Fairy-type Gym Leader of Ballonlea Stadium in both Pokemon Sword and Shield (not version-asymmetric). [Bulbapedia: Opal, via WebSearch snippet]

In game canon, Opal is 88 years old — the oldest Gym Leader in the series — and inherited Ballonlea Gym from her mother roughly 70 years prior to the game's events. [Bulbapedia: Opal, via WebSearch snippet]

Opal's in-game model carries the logo of HIJIKI OKA, a Galar textile company that sponsors Ballonlea Gym (and Opal's chosen successor Bede); her Japanese name 'Popura' derives from poplin fabric, consistent with the textile-sponsor branding. [Bulbapedia: Opal / List of clothing in Pokemon Sword and Shield, via WebSearch snippet]
Opal is nicknamed 'the wizard' in-game for her uncanny ability to predict opponents' moves; she eventually passes the Ballonlea Gym leadership to Bede. [Bulbapedia: Opal, via WebSearch snippet]
Regular print art by take, matching the artist on the rest of the Vivid Voltage Galar Gym Leader Supporters (Bea no. 147, Nessa no. 157). [Bulbapedia: Opal (Vivid Voltage 158), via WebSearch snippet]

Oracle text: 'Flip 2 coins. Search your deck for a number of cards up to the number of heads, put them into your hand, and shuffle your deck.' — a coin-flip tutor Supporter with variance ranging from zero to two cards. [pokemon.com TCG database]
No flavor text printed. [pokemon.com TCG database]
